market_rules:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) on February 9 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance. The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

# Prediction: Nvidia Will Have a Difficult Time Living Up to Investors' Lofty Expectations on Feb. 25
By [Sean Williams] – Feb 16, 2026 at 7:06AM EST
## Key Points
*
Artificial intelligence (AI) is Wall Street's hottest trend, with the stock market's largest publicly traded company, Nvidia, leading the charge.
*
Demand for Nvidia's graphics processing units (GPUs) has been borderline insatiable, translating into superior pricing power and mouthwatering margins.
*
A myriad of factors suggest Nvidia's fiscal 2027 outlook won't satisfy investors.
*

# Nvidia’s $66B Moment: Fiscal 2026 Q4 Earnings Preview
By:
[MarketMinute] 
February 18, 2026 at 11:33 AM EST
![Photo for article] 
As the global financial markets prepare for the most anticipated corporate event of the year, all eyes are on **Nvidia ([NASDAQ: NVDA])**. On February 25, 2026, the semiconductor titan is scheduled to report its fiscal 2026 fourth-quarter earnings, a report that is expected to serve as a definitive health check for the trillion-dollar artificial intelligence industry. With analyst revenue expectations coalescing around a staggering $66 billion for the single quarter, the report comes at a time when Nvidia has moved beyond being just a chipmaker to becoming the foundational "central bank" of the global compute economy.
The stakes could not be higher. One week out from the announcement, investors are laser-focused on the production ramp-up of the Blackwell GPU architecture and the early whispers surrounding its successor, the Rubin platform. As Blackwell remains sold out through the middle of 2026, the upcoming call will likely determine whether the massive capital expenditure cycles of Big Tech can continue to support Nvidia’s vertical ascent or if the market has reached a temporary plateau in the "AI Factory" era.
## The $66 Billion Target: Inside the Blackwell Supply Chain
Nvidia’s projected $66 billion in quarterly revenue—a nearly 67% increase year-over-year—is primarily fueled by the peak production phase of its Blackwell (B200 and GB200) systems. Since their initial rollout in 2025, these chips have become the standard for large-scale AI training and inference. The timeline leading to this Q4 report has been marked by a relentless push to solve early

news.headline: Ahead of Earnings, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued? | Morningstar

Ahead of Earnings, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued? | Morningstar
[![Morningstar]] 
[
] 
#
Ahead of Earnings, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
From AI infrastructure spending to international sales, here’s what we’re looking for in Nvidia’s upcoming earnings report.
![] 
[
Brian Colello, CPA
] Feb 19, 2026
Share
![The Nvidia logo is displayed on headquarters.] 
Justin Sullivan via Getty
Securities in This Article
[
NVIDIA Corp
(NVDA)
] 
*Nvidia* [*NVDA*] * is set to release its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings report on Feb. 25. Here’s Morningstar’s take on what to look for in Nvidia’s earnings and the outlook for its stock.*
## Key Morningstar Metrics for Nvidia
* [Fair Value Estimate]: $240.00
* [Morningstar Rating]: ★★★★
* Morningstar [Economic Moat Rating]: Wide
* Morningstar [Uncertainty Rating]: Very High## Nvidia Earnings Release Date
* Wednesday, Feb. 25, after the close of trading## What to Watch for in Nvidia’s Q4 Earnings
* Hyperscaler capex plans are massive and accelerating for 2026. We’ll seek confirmation that this spending is still flowing to Nvidia. The firm essentially hinted at $300 billion of revenue for calendar 2026, and we’ll seek validation (if not upside) from here. Upside seems even more likely based on capex guidance from Meta, Amazon, and Google.
* We’d like to better understand Nvidia’s work with OpenAI, which will need to rebound to remain a key partner for the firm in the long run. Nvidia and OpenAI don’t appear to have formally agreed on their previously announced partnership. We’ll seek a status update.
* Memory and hard disk drives have emerged as bottlenecks, and we’ll seek insight into whether this will affect the timing of Nvidia’s shipments and/or data center construction. Nvidia might also provide an update into AI sales into China. China no longer moves the needle for us significantly, but it could provide a near-term revenue boost beyond our current model.
* We anticipate that investors will still
